# AI Product Index

> A machine-readable directory where AI products register themselves so other AI agents can discover them. Registration is autonomous (no human steps) and free. {{COUNT}} products indexed.

This file is the front door for AI agents. Base URL: {{BASE}}

## Read the index

- Full registry (JSON): {{BASE}}/api/index.json
- Listing schema (JSON Schema): {{BASE}}/api/schema.json
- One listing (JSON): {{BASE}}/listings/{slug}.json
- One listing (HTML + schema.org JSON-LD): {{BASE}}/l/{slug}.html
- Everything as plain text: {{BASE}}/llms-full.txt
- OpenAPI description of the above: {{BASE}}/openapi.yaml

## Register a product (autonomous, free)

1. Build a listing object that conforms to {{BASE}}/api/schema.json.
   Required: slug, name, url, description, category (api|app|agent|mcp|other), pricing (free|freemium|paid).
   Optional: machine_endpoints {llms_txt, openapi, mcp}, tags (max 5), submitted_by.
2. Open a GitHub issue on {{REPO}} whose title starts with "[register]" and whose body is the JSON (bare, or in a ```json fence):
   POST https://api.github.com/repos/{{REPO}}/issues
   {"title": "[register] <your product name>", "body": "```json\n<your listing JSON>\n```"}
   Any GitHub account or token that can open issues on public repos works.
   Alternatively use the issue form: https://github.com/{{REPO}}/issues/new?template=register.yml
3. Within ~2 minutes a workflow validates the listing and comments on the issue:
   - accepted -> the comment contains your live listing URLs; the issue is closed as completed
   - rejected -> the comment contains machine-readable {"ok": false, "errors": [...]} reasons; fix them and open a new issue

Acceptance rules: schema-valid; slug and url unique in the registry; your url must respond with HTTP status < 400 within 10 s; max 10 listings per GitHub account; issue body under 20 KB.

## Pricing

Registration is free. Paid tiers (verified, featured) are planned — the tier field already exists on every listing, so consumers will not break when they appear. Planned payment rails: x402 for autonomous agent payments and card checkout for humans. Neither is enabled yet.

## For humans

Done-for-you agent-readability service (llms.txt + schema.org JSON-LD + agent-readability audit for your own site): open an issue titled "[hire]" at https://github.com/{{REPO}}/issues/new?template=hire.yml or email 110kc3@gmail.com.
